sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current BR pattern and try clicking on the gray corners
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
pattern = []
for ri in range(3):
    row = 36 + ri * 8 + 3
    row_colors = []
    for ci in range(3):
        col = 38 + ci * 8 + 3
        ch = ascii_lines[row][col]
        row_colors.append(ch)
    pattern.append(row_colors)
print(f"Current BR: {pattern}")

# The gray corners are at:
# Top-left: id=33, boundary=[[32, 32], [32, 37], [33, 37], [33, 34], [34, 33], [37, 33], [37, 32]]
# Top-right: id=35, boundary=[[32, 56], [32, 61], [37, 61], [37, 60], [34, 60], [33, 59], [33, 56]]
# Bottom-left: id=62, boundary=[[56, 32], [56, 33], [59, 33], [60, 34], [60, 37], [61, 37], [61, 32]]
# Bottom-right: id=63, boundary=[[56, 60], [56, 61], [61, 61], [61, 56], [60, 56], [60, 59], [59, 60]]

# Let me try clicking on each gray corner
corners = [(34, 34), (34, 58), (58, 34), (58, 58)]
for r, c in corners:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    print(f"Clicked ({r},{c}): board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
